title: U.S. Bank fined $36 million for freezing unemployment debit cards during COVID-19 pandemic
text: The nation's fifth-largest bank made it extremely difficult for benefit recipients to regain access to frozen accounts, the CFPB found.

title: U.S. Bancorp charged with preventing workers from getting unemployment during COVID
text: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency on Tuesday said they fined U.S. Bancorp USB, +0.41% a combined $36 million for how the bank handled unemployment payments during the COVID-19 pandemic. The CFPB said the bank froze tens of thousands of accounts in a move that kept people from collecting their unemployment benefits.

title: US OCC fines U.S. Bank $15 million for unfair practices - statement
text: The U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency assessed a $15 million civil money penalty against U.S. Bank for violations of law relating to its administration of a prepaid card program to distribute public unemployment insurance benefit payments, the OCC said on Tuesday.
